Here are all the hardcoded email addresses found in the webapp source code:

| # | File | Line | Email Address |
|---|------|------|---------------|
| 1 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 9 | `alice@example.com` |
| 2 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 10 | `bob@example.com` |
| 3 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 11 | `charlie@example.com` |

**Summary:** All three hardcoded emails are located in `UsersController.scala` and appear to be **hardcoded seed/test user data** — specifically a list of `User` objects with fictitious names (Alice, Bob, Charlie) and placeholder `@example.com` addresses. This is a common code smell; these records should ideally be moved to a database seed file, a configuration file, or a test fixture rather than being embedded directly in application source code.
